target.name: Koloma
target.founder: Tom Darrah, Pete Johnson, Paul Harraka

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $245M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Koloma, a Series B climate and energy company founded by Tom Darrah, Pete Johnson, and Paul Harraka, has raised $245M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables (KoBold Metals at $3.0B Series C, Electric Hydrogen at $1.0B Series C, and Amogy at $700M Series C), alongside a broader universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable in the climate-tech space.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $245M total raised proxy, yielding $466M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-sector comparables produces a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range spanning $700M to $3.0B. These methods reflect materially different assumptions about Koloma's relative standing and exit potential within its cohort.

The two-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $396M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $733M. The substantial spread reflects the wide dispersion in recent Series C outcomes for climate-tech peers and inherent uncertainty around Koloma's commercial trajectory relative to these benchmarks. Key sensitivities include the comparability of peer business models, the timing and scale of capital efficiency improvements typical at this stage, and near-term fundraising dynamics in frontier energy.
